title: AST-aware diffs — research + spike difftastic integration
task:
EditRenderer / ApplyPatchRenderer today render line-level diffs. AST-aware diffs (difftastic, diffsitter) use tree-sitter to identify which AST nodes actually changed and highlight only those — so renaming a variable shows the variable token coloured, not the entire line. This is qualitatively better for refactors and reviews.

This is a RESEARCH + SPIKE task, not a full implementation. Goal: determine feasibility, identify integration points, prototype the simplest path that produces a visible AST-aware diff for a single edit case. Real wiring is a follow-up task.

requirements.description: Survey: is difftastic available as a Rust crate? Try `cargo search difftastic` and check crates.io. If yes, note the crate name + version + API surface (does it expose a programmatic diff function, or only the CLI?). If no library API, document the alternative: shell out to the difftastic binary, or use tree-sitter directly. Also check diffsitter (sister project). Write findings to docs/reports/ast-aware-diff-feasibility.md.
requirements.acceptance: A research doc at docs/reports/ast-aware-diff-feasibility.md naming the available crates, their APIs, the languages they support, and the integration cost.

requirements.description: If a usable library is found: prototype a single EditRenderer test path that takes old_string + new_string for a Rust snippet, runs the AST diff, and produces output where only the changed tokens are highlighted (rest of the line is dim or plain). Output goes through ContentBlock::Diff (or a new ContentBlock::AstDiff variant). The prototype is a `#[cfg(test)]` test or a small example binary — NOT wired into the main render path yet.
requirements.acceptance: A working test or example demonstrating an AST-aware diff for a one-line Rust change (e.g. rename a variable). The diff visibly highlights only the changed identifier, not the surrounding context.

requirements.description:
Data flow analysis. The edit tool currently passes old_string + new_string as the diff source. For AST diffing we need surrounding context (at least the containing function or block) to give tree-sitter enough to parse meaningfully. Document the options:
(a) The renderer reads the file from disk before/after the edit. Architecturally dubious (renderers are pure) but works without changing the tool.
(b) The edit tool emits more context in its result (old_block + new_block, snapshotted by the tool itself). Cleaner but requires tool-side changes.
(c) A new intermediate layer caches file content per edit and feeds the renderer.
Recommendation: short-term (b) by adding `result.ast_diff = {old_block, new_block, language}` to the edit tool's blast_radius output. Long-term separation of concerns leaves the renderer pure and the tool authoritative.
requirements.acceptance: Recommendation captured in the docs/reports doc with rationale + estimated effort for each option.

requirements.description: Gates pass: cargo check; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings; cargo test (full workspace if a new dep was added); grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files returns zero.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/tools/rich/edit.rs — EditRenderer.
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/content.rs — ContentBlock + render_diff pipeline.
- crates/norn/src/tools/edit.rs — EditTool emitting result.blast_radius.

WORKSPACE DEPS:
- tree-sitter is already used in libyggd (workspace dep — check Cargo.toml). Reusing it would be ideal.
- libyggd has AST plumbing already (see crates/libyggd/) — there might be diff infrastructure we can lean on instead of pulling in difftastic.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — don't reinvent:
- ContentBlock pipeline.
- syntect highlighting (we'd still use this for the unchanged tokens).
- The edit tool's result shape — extend rather than rewrite.

STANDARDS: this is research + a spike, NOT a full feature. The deliverable is a feasibility doc + a prototype, not a production-ready renderer. Don't add a heavy dep to the production workspace without a clear plan.
